sources.label: Itron Publishes 2025 Corporate Sustainability Report (Jun 3, 2026 - pre-cutoff)
Itron to deploy 10,000 smart water meters in Corsica (Jun 2, 2026 - pre-cutoff)
ITRI analyst ratings and price targets - last action Stephens $130 PT on May 20, 2026; nothing new in June
Itron investor relations (no new releases since Jun 6)

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6 and today's -0.26% tape is noise, so nothing justifies moving again after yesterday's hold. On the latent-nodes lens the setup quietly improves at the margin: -13.6% YTD with the grid-telemetry thesis fully intact (Corsica AMI win Jun 2, sell-side PTs $118-136 vs $81.59) keeps the asymmetry favorable with zero crowding.
verdicts.bull: AI datacenter load growth forces utilities into demand response and granular grid visibility, and Itron's installed endpoint base plus its grid-edge intelligence software are the cheapest existing rails for that; consensus targets sit 45-65% above spot on an under-owned $3.6B name.
verdicts.neutral: Steady but routine AMI bookings (Corsica-type wins) and a clean balance sheet; the stock likely chops sideways until backlog conversion or a marquee grid-AI contract proves reacceleration.
verdicts.bear: Itron is still a meter vendor at heart: utility capex timing slips, AMI replacement cycles lengthen, and the AI-power narrative accrues to generation and electrical-equipment names rather than telemetry, leaving ITRI a low-growth industrial.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A large utility AMI/grid-edge intelligence award or Q2 earnings (late July) showing bookings reacceleration tied to datacenter-driven grid spend.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued utility order-timing slippage keeping the stock dead money despite the intact thesis.
